Title: Brandywine Remote Issues Again
Notes:
Ticket Summary: VPN Connection Issue (CBI - Jason)

Issue:
Jason is unable to connect to the Sophos VPN. The VPN profile was verified on the firewall and credentials were confirmed to be correct. However, the issue persists due to the requirement for a six-digit OTP linked to Jason's user account.

Actions Taken:

  • Verified the VPN profile and credentials.

  • Checked the firewall configuration to ensure the profile was correct.

  • Discussed the issue with Jason, who is currently in a meeting and unable to provide the OTP at this time.

Next Steps:

  • Follow up with Jason after his meeting to retrieve the six-digit OTP and complete the VPN connection setup.

Title: Device Non-Compliant Due to Missing Patches CBILT06, & CBILT12
Notes:
Devices CBILT06 and CBILT12 were identified as non-compliant due to missing patches.

Logged in to the ManageEngine portal and verified pending updates on both systems. Proceeded with patch installation for the following updates:

  • Google Chrome (x64) – Versions 146.0.7680.153 / 146.0.7680.154
  • Microsoft 365 Apps for Enterprise (Current Channel, x64) – Version 2602 (Build 19725.20190)

After installation, performed a manual patch scan on both systems to verify successful deployment and to clear the pending patch status.

Post validation, confirmed that all patches have been successfully applied and no high vulnerabilities remain. Both systems are now compliant as per patch management policy.

Title: Brandywine Remote Issues Again
Notes:
Ticket Summary:

Received an email from Jason regarding an issue accessing the CI application on the 91 VM. Jason reported that the application was failing when attempting to connect using his Guardian setup.

Verified that the credentials, connection details, and node configuration were correct. Initial troubleshooting included removing and reconfiguring the VPN connection; however, the issue persisted.

Performed additional steps including disabling and re-enabling the user account, followed by a complete uninstall and reinstall of the application on the endpoint.

After reinstallation, Jason tested access again and was able to successfully connect to the application. Functionality was confirmed, and the issue is now resolved.
